docstring:
/**
 * @brief Compiler-generated initialization stub invoked at program startup.
 *
 * This function is emitted automatically by the toolchain (placed in the
 * .init_array / .ctors section) and is run before main() as part of the C
 * runtime startup sequence. Its sole job is to delegate to
 * register_tm_clones(), which sets up Transactional Memory clone tables
 * when supported.
 *
 * @note This is not user code; it is produced by GCC and should not be
 *       called or modified directly. It takes no arguments and returns
 *       no value.
 */
assumptions: This is the standard glibc crt frame_dummy function, which is typically compiled to a tail call into register_tm_clones(); the pseudocode body shown actually appears to be the body of register_tm_clones itself (computes (__TMC_END__ - __TMC_END__)/8 ... ), but the prototype labels it frame_dummy.
Treated the indirect call through __TMC_END__[0] and the size/2 computation as the well-known register_tm_clones idiom rather than reproducing it literally.
Dropped optimizer artefacts: shifted-by-63 sign correction for signed division by 8, and the unreachable goto L_18a0 after return.
divergences: [low] other: The pseudocode shown appears to be the body of register_tm_clones (computes (__TMC_END__ - __TMC_END__)/8/2 and indirect-calls *__TMC_END__) rather than frame_dummy proper. The rewrite collapses this to a single tail call to register_tm_clones(), which is the standard glibc crt idiom and behaviorally equivalent.
[low] control_flow: Unreachable goto after return was dropped. No observable behavior change.
